L01.rationale: Transit drivers are a fixed shuttle rota of 4 every day and do not scale with outbound volume, so the plan's nudges to 4.2/4.5 on big outbound days should be ignored.

L02.rationale: Co-Packing is a fixed cell at exactly 4 crew regardless of outbound volume; the optimiser wrongly scales it up.

L03.rationale: Picking is overstaffed; ran ~12-15% under recommended pickers all week and still cleared every wave, so trim picker person-days about 12%.

L04.rationale: Receiving needs +1 on Mondays because it's the heaviest inbound day and the plan only staffs the weekday baseline.

L05.rationale: Loading runs tight on payday Mondays (last Monday of month), so add +1 loader.

L06.rationale: On genuinely low-volume days (picks under ~7,000) one picker idles, so trim one picker.

L07.rationale: On the first working day after any closure, inbound backlog requires +1 each on Receiving and Putaway.

L08.rationale: Late-summer (W30–W33) volumes drop due to heat/holidays, so the whole operative plan is overstaffed by ~15% and should be cut.

L09.rationale: Summer heat slows the floor (pickers especially), so we needed more hours not fewer — do not blindly cut for summer.

L10.rationale: Co-Packing line has run a fixed crew of exactly 4 for about 8 weeks straight, applied every cycle.

L11.rationale: After pick-by-light went live on 24.08, Picking runs ~25% under plan, a deeper trim than the prior ~12%.

L12.rationale: Two weeks after pick-by-light, picking runs ~25–30% under plan, so cut Picking by ~27%, retiring the stale 12% figure.

L13.rationale: On heavy-inbound days (>~2,000 pallets) VNA replenishment needs a second person.

L14.rationale: Flu-season ramp is pulling outbound up week over week; the optimiser lags the trend and under-plans Staging and Loading, so plan ahead of the forecast.

L15.rationale: Autumn ramp is pushing picks up ~8% and climbing into October, leaving outbound roles short — staff up to the rising trend rather than the lagging forecast.
